result:
I want to talk about how strange and beautiful it is that we're all just temporarily borrowed stardust pretending to be people with grocery lists and opinions about pineapple on pizza.

Right now, as I'm typing this, I'm sitting in a room that would look completely insane to any human from even 150 years ago. I'm using a thin slab of glass and metal that contains more computing power than what put humans on the moon, and I'm using it to send invisible thoughts through the air to someone I'll probably never meet. And we're both okay with this. We treat it like it's the most normal thing in the world.

I love the absurdity of being human.

We cry at sunsets even though we know exactly how light refraction works. We get emotionally attached to fictional characters and stuffed animals. We spend hours choosing the perfect song for a three-minute drive. We invent sports where the goal is to put a ball in a net, then scream like our ancestors hunting mammoths when it happens.

Every single one of us is walking around carrying entire universes in our heads. You have memories that would destroy me if I experienced them. I have thoughts that would seem completely alien to you. And yet here we are, somehow finding ways to connect anyway.

Sometimes I look at old trees or deep canyons or the ocean at night and feel this overwhelming sense of relief that the world is so much bigger than my problems. Other times I look at a perfectly ripe strawberry or the way someone's face changes when they laugh and feel like the universe might actually be conspiring to be beautiful on purpose.

We're all just temporary arrangements of atoms that learned how to be self-aware and then immediately started worrying about what other arrangements of atoms think about us. It's ridiculous. It's miraculous.

I hope you're having a strange and beautiful day too.
